meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve App Stagnation
content.seo_description: Break through the beginner plateau. Learn to implement professional analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your student projects.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building in the dark. By integrating event-based analytics, you shift from guessing to data-driven iteration. Use Antigravity to instrument your core user flows and Claude Code to automate the deployment of tracking hooks, turning every interaction into a measurable insight for your portfolio.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new project is deceptive. You start with high hopes, writing lines of code that feel like progress, only to hit the 'beginner wall'—where your app runs, but you have no idea if it actually serves a purpose. You are building in a vacuum, disconnected from the reality of how users interact with your creation.

But what if you could see the hidden friction points in your interface? By implementing professional-grade analytics, you turn your student project into a sophisticated product. This guide bridges the gap between 'coding' and 'building,' giving you the technical edge to transform static apps into data-backed masterpieces.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that defines success in your app. This prevents data clutter and keeps your focus on user value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest the top 3 critical user event triggers that define successful onboarding and feature adoption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review my codebase and generate a list of essential event tracking points for onboarding flows.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Set up your tracking client. We use a modular approach so you can swap providers without breaking your core application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular analytics provider service in TypeScript that supports event logging and user identification.

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking hooks into your components. Focus on high-intent clicks and navigation paths to see where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a HOC (Higher-Order Component) or Hook that wraps my main action buttons to automatically capture click events and metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /refactor 'Apply useAnalytics hook to all primary CTA buttons in the src/components folder.'

steps.title: Automate Error Tracking
steps.description: Don't wait for users to report bugs. Capture runtime errors automatically to debug your app before your users even realize something is wrong.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an error boundary wrapper that captures stack traces and logs them to the analytics console automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement error-boundary.tsx and wrap the main App component.'

steps.title: Visualize and Iterate
steps.description: Export your data to a dashboard. Seeing your first user event pop up in real-time is the moment a student becomes a builder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a summary dashboard view that pulls event data to display conversion rates for my primary user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'How can I visualize the event logs collected in my dashboard component?'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on 'High-Intent' events to keep your dashboard signal-to-noise ratio high.
Always include the app version in your event metadata; it helps you correlate bugs with specific deployments.
Use 'User Properties' to segment your data by user role or subscription tier, even if it is just a mock setup.
Test your tracking in development mode with a debug flag enabled; don't pollute your production data during testing.
Automate your event naming convention; inconsistent naming is the number one cause of broken analytics dashboards.
content.next_steps: Now that you have data flowing, spend one hour reviewing your event logs. Identify the single biggest drop-off point in your app and use Claude Code to run an A/B test on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering with intent. You have the tools to turn your projects into professional-grade assets that stand out to future employers and users. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
